name: Draft Slot Tracker
description: Searchable web tracker of every MLB draft signing 2018-2026 (~5,500 picks): bonus vs slot value, over/under-slot diff, share of team bonus pool, agency, with filters for year, round range, position (RHP/LHP split), college vs HS, power conference, age at draft, bonus range, and agency. Exports the filtered view to CSV; a companion Node script turns that export into a styled one-page PDF report.
stack: Static HTML/CSS/JS (single index.html, no framework, no build step)
Node + headless Chrome (make-pdf.js, local PDF reports)
hosting: GitHub Pages, deployed automatically from main of Stadium-Ventures/draft-slot-tracker
access: Public URL, no auth — repo and site are public. On-page brand reads "SV Draft Slots".

inputs: data.csv committed in the repo — a static snapshot of MLB draft signings 2018-2026 (~5,500 rows: pick, player, bonus, slot value, position, bats/throws, school/conference, birthdate, agency). No live feed; the page fetches this file client-side.

2026 rows (bonus, slot, agency) were seeded one-time from the MLB Stats API draft feed (https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/draft/2026 — pickValue/signingBonus per pick) joined with sv-draft-day's public/data/composite.json (player -> agent) for agency. Bonuses and agencies fill in as signings happen through the Aug 2026 deadline (343/613 bonuses and 175/613 agencies populated as of the 2026-07-23 pull) — refreshing means re-pulling both sources and re-merging by hand, there is no script in this repo for it yet.
outputs: CSV download of the current filtered view (Export CSV button on the site)
One-page PDF reports generated locally: node make-pdf.js <export.csv> [title] (renders via headless Google Chrome)

links.label: Live site
links.note: MLB Draft Slot Tracker (2018-2026). Search a player or filter, then Export CSV for report material.

monitoring: None needed — static GitHub Pages site with no scheduled jobs. Only failure mode is a Pages build failure on push, visible in the repo's Actions tab ("pages build and deployment").
knownIssues: 2026 bonus/agency coverage is partial and will go stale again: only signed picks have a bonus (343/613 as of 2026-07-23) and only picks matched to sv-draft-day's composite rankings have an agency (175/613); both need a manual re-pull + re-merge as more players sign through the deadline (👤 Mike DeCicco)
notes: Repo was transferred from mdecicco-SV/draft-slot-tracker to the Stadium-Ventures org; the old name is now a GitHub redirect, so either remote URL works but Stadium-Ventures/draft-slot-tracker is canonical. The original source/export behind the 2018-2025 rows (columns suggest an MLB draft database export) is not documented in-repo, so refreshing that historical data requires re-deriving that pull. The 2026 rows use a different, now-documented pipeline (MLB Stats API + sv-draft-day composite.json) — see pipes.inputs and the runbook.
runbook: Clone Stadium-Ventures/draft-slot-tracker — no dependencies, no env vars, no secrets.
Run locally: python3 -m http.server 8000 in the repo root, then open http://localhost:8000 (index.html fetches data.csv, so opening the file directly via file:// will not load data).
Update historical data: edit data.csv (header row defines the schema: phase,year,draftRound,overall,...,bonus,slot_bonus,difference,agency), commit, push to main.

Refresh 2026 bonus/agency data: pull https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/draft/2026 for pickValue (slot) and signingBonus per pick, and Stadium-Ventures/sv-draft-day's public/data/composite.json for player -> agent; join by player name and overwrite the 2026 rows in data.csv (round labels: fold any non-numeric pickRound like PPI/CB-A/CB-B/SUP-2/2C/4C into the nearest preceding numeric round, matching how prior years' comp picks are recorded). Commit, push to main.
Deploy: push to main — GitHub Pages rebuilds automatically in ~1 minute (watch the "pages build and deployment" run in the repo's Actions tab).
PDF report: use the site's Export CSV button, then node make-pdf.js <export.csv> [title] [output.pdf] — requires Google Chrome at the default macOS path, or set CHROME=/path/to/chrome.
First troubleshooting step if the site looks stale or broken: check the latest "pages build and deployment" run under the repo's Actions tab.
